title: obione angularis
intro: Obione angularis, also known as Red Sandwort, is a perennial plant species in the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its reddish-purple flowers and needle-like leaves. Traditionally, Red Sandwort has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing swelling and pain, and supporting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Red Sandwort is often combined with other herbs to create topical creams, salves, and ointments that can be applied directly to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
